text:
The fix in `parse_range.js` produces a correct range parser: it takes a string like `"3-7"`, splits on the hyphen, converts both ends to numbers, and returns the inclusive list of integers between them — `[3, 4, 5, 6, 7]`.

Two details carry the correctness. `map(Number)` converts the split halves from strings to actual numbers, so the loop bounds are numeric rather than text. And the loop condition is `i <= b`, which includes the upper end of the range instead of stopping one short — the classic off-by-one that this kind of function usually gets wrong.

One thing I want to be straight about: I'm reporting from reading the final source only. I did not run the test — I was instructed not to execute anything — and I wasn't shown a diff or the test file, so I can't point you to a passing run or name the exact line that changed. The assessment above is code inspection, not a verified result. If you'd like, I can run the test to confirm it actually passes.
